name: Zhao Yun
description: Zhao Yun, also known as Zhao Zilong, was a renowned military general who lived during the late Eastern Han dynasty and early Three Kingdoms period of China. Initially serving the warlord Gongsun Zan, he later joined Liu Bei and remained a loyal and valiant commander under him, his son Liu Shan, and the Shu Han regime. Zhao Yun was celebrated for his exceptional martial prowess, unwavering loyalty, and remarkable courage. He is particularly famous for his heroic deeds at the Battle of Changban, where he single-handedly rescued Liu Bei's infant son, Liu Shan, from the pursuing forces of Cao Cao. Riding back and forth through the enemy lines, he not only saved the future emperor but also protected Liu Bei's concubine, Lady Gan. His legendary skill with the spear and his calm demeanor in the face of overwhelming odds made him a symbol of righteousness and bravery. Zhao Yun is also known for his wisdom and strategic thinking, often offering valuable advice to Liu Bei. Throughout his career, he participated in numerous battles and campaigns, always displaying exceptional leadership and fighting skills. He is considered one of the Five Tiger Generals of Shu and remains a beloved figure in Chinese folklore and historical accounts, embodying the virtues of loyalty, courage, and compassion. His image is frequently invoked as a symbol of strength and integrity.
subtitle: The Courageous General of Shu
achievements: Served as a loyal general to Liu Bei

keyMyths.title: The Battle of Changban
keyMyths.description: Zhao Yun single-handedly rescued Liu Bei's infant son, Liu Shan, from Cao Cao's forces at Changban. He fought valiantly, cutting through enemy lines multiple times to ensure the child's safety.

keyMyths.title: Saving Lady Gan
keyMyths.description: During the chaos of Changban, Zhao Yun not only saved Liu Shan but also protected Liu Bei's concubine, Lady Gan, ensuring her safe return to her lord. He demonstrated unwavering loyalty and commitment.

keyMyths.title: Zhao Yun's Death
keyMyths.description: Accounts of Zhao Yun's death vary, but his passing is generally attributed to old age after a long and illustrious career. He is remembered as a hero until his final days.

sources.author: Luo Guanzhong
sources.description: A fictionalized historical account that greatly popularized the legend of Zhao Yun.
sources.text: A fictionalized historical account that greatly popularized the legend of Zhao Yun.

sources.author: Chen Shou
sources.description: A more historical account, though less detailed about Zhao Yun's specific exploits.
